articles.title_en: How drones came to decide the war in Ukraine, and why no army has found the weapon that stops them
articles.lead_en: Ukrainian strikes on Russian oil refineries and transport well behind the front lines — a hundred kilometers away or more — have forced Russia’s Defense Ministry to launch an overhaul of the country’s air defenses. Defense Minister Andrei Belousov has promised that by November, Russia will complete a new integrated, layered air defense system capable of countering drones across the country’s entire territory, from the front lines to the deep rear.

articles.title_en: Crimea’s wine industry is collapsing under fuel shortages and power outages, growers say
articles.lead_en: Crimea continues to face a fuel and energy crisis triggered by Ukrainian strikes aimed at cutting the annexed peninsula off from mainland Russia. The power outages and fuel shortages have taken a severe toll on the region’s wine industry, Forbes Russia reported after speaking with local wine producers.

articles.title_en: Meet the new app helping Russians find gas amid the country’s fuel crisis
articles.lead_en: Against the backdrop of Russia’s fuel crisis, large companies like Yandex and Sberbank, as well as individual enthusiasts, have been developing tools to help Russians find gas stations that still have fuel. A few weeks ago, an app called “GdeBENZ” appeared in the App Store and RuStore, and it became more popular than its larger, better-known counterpart, Yandex Zapravki, with remarkable speed.
